title: Protect Memory: - Verify Info: Check Facts Before Trusting New Details About an Event
subtitle: Guard Against Misinformation
description:
To protect memory:
- Verify info: Check facts before trusting new details about an event.
- Write it down: Record key facts right after an event to lock them in.
- Question changes: Be skeptical of info that seems to alter your original memory.

Example: If you hear a new version of a story, cross-check it with your original notes or memory.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

howTo.title: Anchor key facts immediately
howTo.summary: Write down 3-6 core facts about an event within 10 minutes to preserve your memory before external influences alter it.

howTo.title: Verify conflicting details
howTo.summary: When new information contradicts your notes, ask one clarifying question to check the original facts and prevent misinformation.

howTo.title: Record provenance and confidence
howTo.summary: If you receive new information later, document its source and your confidence level to maintain clarity in your memory.

howTo.title: Conduct a verification routine
howTo.summary: Within 24-72 hours, cross-check your notes with objective sources and corroborate with others to ensure accuracy.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and reinforce the habit. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/verify-facts-protect-memory/.
